original: Battle of Strasbourg: Roman Caesar Julian defeats the Alamanni tribal confederation led by King Chnodomar, who is captured and taken to Rome in chains
Children's crusade under Nicolas (10) reaches Genoa
Dutch city of Ommen receives city rights and fortification rights from Archbishop of Utrecht Otto III
Antipope Nicholas V, having obtained assurance of pardon, presents a confession of his sins to Pope John XXII at Avignon, who absolves him
Countess Jacoba of Bavaria escapes from jail
Battle of Sapienza: Turkish fleet defeats Venetians
Conquistador Diego Velázquez de Cuéllar founds Havana in modern-day Cuba on the southern coast of the island; it is moved to its current location in 1519
The Honourable Artillery Company, the oldest surviving regiment in the British Army, and the second most senior, is formed
Explorer Hernando de Alarcón travels up the Colorado River
Iconoclastic fury begins in the Dutch province of Utrecht
Battle of Alcantara: Spain defeats Portugal
Slavery begins in mainland British colonies of North America when the first known African captives (approximately 20) land at Point Comfort, Virginia, before being sold or traded into servitude
Assault on Sultan of Mataram in Batavia
Siege of Deal Castle ends in the surrender of Royalist supporters to Parliamentarians during the Second English Civil War
Battle of Arras: Louis de Bourbon, Prince of Condé, fails to take Arras and withdraws to Cambrai
Battle of Charleroi: Spanish and English armies chase the French
Montreal is taken by the Iroquois
Hundreds of French colonists arrive in Louisiana and found New Orleans
Curaçao slave opponents return to St. Christopher
Alicia Thornton becomes the first female jockey in England, riding at Knavesmire in Yorkshire
British forces destroy the Library of Congress, which contains 3,000 books (War of 1812)
Uruguay declares independence from Brazil (National Day)
President Jackson offers to buy Texas, but the Mexican government refuses
Belgium revolts against the Netherlands and begins the Belgian Revolution

The Tubular Victoria Bridge across the St. Lawrence River at Montreal is officially opened by the Prince of Wales, an engineering wonder of the day, as it is the longest bridge in the world at that time, measuring 2 km
US Secretary of War authorizes Gen. Rufus Saxton to arm 5,000 enslaved people
Battle of Petersburg: Battle of Reams Station
Combination rail and ferry service is available from San Francisco to Alameda
First international polo match (US vs. England)
Balinese troops assault Dutch army; 97 are killed (assault ends the following day)
Japanese scientist Shibasaburo Kitasato discovers the infectious agent of the bubonic plague and publishes his findings in The Lancet
700 Greeks and 15 Englishmen are slaughtered by the Turks in Heraklion, Crete
Allan Winter wins the first $50,000 trotting race in the US
National Association of Colored Nurses forms in New York City, and Martha Minerva Franklin of Connecticut is elected president
Yellow Cab is founded
Different nationalities battle with one another in Macedonia
First time an aircraft recovers from a spin
The Kuomintang, the Chinese nationalist party, is founded
Belgian offensive in Antwerp, Belgium, until August 26, 1914
German troops in occupied Belgium begin the six-week "Sack of Louvain," destroying historical buildings and killing hundreds of civilians
German troops march into France and push the French army to Sedan
German Zeppelins bomb Antwerp, Belgium, killing 10
The US Department of the Interior forms the National Park Service
First scheduled passenger service by airplane (Paris to London)
American swimmer Norman Ross wins his first of three gold medals at the Antwerp Olympics in dominating the men's 1,500 m freestyle; also wins the 400 m freestyle and 4 x 200 m freestyle relay
Russia suffers a decisive defeat in the Battle of Warsaw against Poland
Swedish divers sweep the medals in the men's plain high diving event at the Antwerp Olympics as Arvid Wallman wins gold ahead of teammates Nils Skoglund and John Jansson
The first skirmishes of the Battle of Blair Mountain, a civil uprising in Logan County, West Virginia
US signs a peace treaty with Germany
Yankees pitcher Harry Harper hits three batters in an inning, tying the record
Cubs beat Phillies 26-23 in the highest-scoring Major League game
International maritime treaty is drafted
MLB Washington SenatorWalter Johnsonpitches his second no-hitter, beating the Browns 2-0 in 7 innings
Brotherhood of Sleeping Car Porters organizes in Harlem, New York
Last Belgian troops vacate Duisburg
Pavlos Koundouris becomes President of Greece
Tri-City Rugby Football Union forms, consisting of Moose Jaw, Regina, and Winnipeg
Jan van Houten (25) cycles a world record distance of 44.588 km in an hour, at the velodrome in Maasniel, Netherlands
Odorless cornstarch is patented in the US by Ralph W. Kerr
Three Braves hit twice in an inning, each getting two hits
The Japanese fleet blockades the Chinese coast
First British night bombing of Germany in Berlin
Lithuania, Latvia, and Estonia are incorporated into the Soviet Union
British and Soviet troops attack pro-German Iran
German troops conquer Novgorod, Leningrad
SS begins transporting Jews from Maastricht
10th NFL Chicago All-Star Game: All-Stars 27, Washington 7 (48,471 attendees)
German occupiers impose a 72-hour work week
The Red Army under Gen. Vatutin recaptures Akhtyrka
US forces overrun New Georgia in the Solomon Islands during WWII
US Army XII Corps reaches Troyes, France
Jewish immigrants are permitted to leave Mauritius for Palestine
Marion Carl in D-558-I sets a world aircraft speed record of 1,047 km/h
US President Harry Truman orders the army to take control of the railroads to avert a strike
Cleveland Indians win 16th consecutive home game
Detroit Tiger Virgil Trucks throws his second no-hitter of the year, beating the Yankees 1-0
Puerto Rico becomes a US commonwealth
Ivan Filin comes third in the Bern Marathon; he enters the stadium first but turns the wrong way and is overtaken by two other runners
16th Venice Film Festival: "Ordet" directed by Carl Theodor Dreyer wins Golden Lion
Last Soviet forces leave Austria
To make room for Enos Slaughter, the NY Yankees releasePhil Rizzuto
Prince Souvanna Phouma forms a government in Laos with the Pathet Lao
AFL begins placing players' names on the backs of their jerseys
XVII Summer Olympic Games open at Stadio Olimpico in Rome, Italy
Brazilian president Janio Quadros resigns
USSR performs a nuclear test at Novaya Zemlya, USSR
USSR performs a nuclear test at the Semipalatinsk Test Site in northeast Kazakhstan
Paul McCartneyis fined £31 and given a one-year suspended license for speeding
Singapore limits imports from Netherlands due to Indonesian aggression
Minnesota Twin Dean Chance's second no-hitter of the month beats Cleveland 2-1
Paraguay adopts its constitution
Train crash in Beesd, Netherlands; two people die
NY Yankees outfielderRocky Colavitopitches 2/3 of an inning and beats the Tigers 6-5; he plays right field in the second game and hits a home run
The Detroit Lions beat the Boston Patriots 22-9 in Montreal (NFL exhibition game)
Butch Trucks, drummer of the Allman Brothers, breaks his leg in a car crash
France performs a nuclear test at Mururoa Atoll
Zambia adopts a constitution
France performs nuclear test at Mururoa Atoll
LA Aztecs defeat Miami Toros to win the NASL Cup
Harm Wiersma becomes world checkers champion
Yankees beat Twins 5-4 in 19 innings
MLB baseball umpires stage a one-day strike
California Angels trounce Toronto Blue Jays 24-2
Somali adopts a constitution
MLB Texas Rangers pitcherFerguson Jenkinsis arrested for possession of drugs
Jeff Schwartz sets a solo record for trampoline bouncing, jumping for 246 hours (more than 10 consecutive days)
Triple-A baseball's Louisville Redbirds break the one-million-fan milestone
US and USSR sign a $10 billion grain pact
French airship capsizes
USSR performs an underground nuclear test
STS 51-I is scrubbed at T-5 minutes because of an onboard computer problem
A'sMark McGwirehits his first major league home run
Dow Jones Industrial Average reaches a record of 2722.42
Madonnasings in Rotterdam, Netherlands
Challenger Center opens its classroom doors in Houston
Iran and Iraq begin talks to end their eight-year war
NASA launches space vehicle S-214
Serious fire destroys historic center of Lisbon
After a 12-year, 4-billion-mile journey, Voyager 2 flies over the cloud tops of Neptune and its moon Triton, sending back photographs of swamps
Mayumi Moriyama becomes Japan's first female cabinet secretary
Li Hui Rong of China sets the women's triple jump record of 47 ft 8½ in (14.54 m)
43rd Emmy Awards: "LA Law," "Cheers," Kirstie Alley, and Patricia Wettig win
Cubs Doug Dascenzo commits his first career error after 242 games
Krisztina Egerszegi swims the 200 m backstroke in a world record time of 2:06.62
Mitch Voges wins the 91st US Golf Amateur Championship
Norway and Denmark recognize the independence of the former USSR Baltic republics
The Byelorussian SSR declares its independence
Wanda Panfil wins the third Women's World Championship Marathon in 2:29:53
Gloucestershire cricketerAndrew Symondsscores 254 against Glamorgan, including a then first-class record of 16 sixes
Indians' José Mesa fails in a save attempt after 38 consecutive saves
Egon Krenz, former East German leader, is convicted for a shoot-to-kill Berlin Wall policy
Singer Aaliyah and eight others are killed in a plane crash in the Bahamas
Tłı̨chǫ land claims agreement is signed between the Tłı̨chǫ First Nations and the Canadian federal government in Rae-Edzo (now called Behchokǫ̀)
Cuba beats Australia 6-2 to win the baseball gold medal at the Athens Olympics; Japan takes bronze; the US does not qualify
Emanuel Rego and Ricardo Santos win Brazil's first gold medal in men's beach volleyball with a 21–16, 21–15 victory over the Spanish pair Javier Bosma and Pablo Herrera
Gal Fridman becomes Israel's first Olympic gold medalist when he wins the men's Mistral One Design windsurfing event at the Athens Games
Greek athlete Fani Chalkia comes out of retirement to compete in her home Olympics in Athens and wins the 400 m hurdles gold medal in 52.82
Olga Kuzenkova of Russia throws an Olympic record 75.02 m to win the women's hammer throw gold medal at the Athens Games
Hyperion, the world's tallest living tree, a redwood standing 115.55 m (379.1 ft), is discovered by naturalists Chris Atkins and Michael Taylor in Redwood National and State Parks, California
First Women's Snow Polo World Championship takes place in Bariloche, Argentina

Panic caused by escaped crocodile being smuggled aboard domestic Filair flight leads to crash of Let L-410 Turbolet passenger aircraft near Bandundu Airport, Democratic Republic of the Congo, killing all but one of the 21 passengers and crew
330 people are killed as a result of conflict in the Syrian Civil War
39 people are killed and 80 are injured after a gas leak in a northern Venezuelan refinery
85,000 people are displaced by severe floods in Myanmar
Voyager 1 spacecraft becomes the first spacecraft to enter interstellar space (launched in 1977)
41 people are killed in a wave of bomb attacks across Iraq
Four people are killed and 25 are injured after a train derails in Tabasco, Mexico
66th Primetime Emmy Awards, hosted bySeth Meyers, are broadcast on NBC
Category 4 Hurricane Harvey makes landfall in Texas northeast of Corpus Christi with 130 mph winds
Indian spiritual leader Gurmeet Ram Rahim Singh is convicted of raping two of his followers in Panchkula
Samsung heir Lee Jae-yong is jailed for five years for corruption in South Korea
Afghan Islamic State leader Abu Saad Erhabi and 10 others are killed in an airstrike in the province of Nangarhar, Afghanistan
Double Dave, a rare two-headed timber rattlesnake, is found in Pine Barrens forest, New Jersey
Chicago White Sox pitcher Lucas Giolito no-hits the Pittsburgh Pirates, 4-0 at Guaranteed Rate Field, Chicago
England's James Anderson becomes the first fast bowler to reach 600 wickets in Test cricket as Pakistan batsman Azhar Ali is caught at first slip on the 5th day of the 3rd Test at Southampton
California votes to ban the sale of all new gasoline-powered cars by 2035
Arba’at Dam collapses after torrential rain in Sudan’s northwestern Red Sea State, killing at least 30 people, with hundreds missing
Israel and Hezbollah exchange rocket and drone attacks in an escalation of conflict in southern Lebanon, killing four people
Gokteik, a historic Myanmar bridge, is destroyed during fighting between the military junta and anti-coup armed groups, who blame each other for the destruction
Haboob dust storm swallows parts of metro Phoenix, Arizona, creating near-zero visibility, followed by severe thunderstorms causing wind damage and widespread power outages
Thousands protest against housing allowances for MPs outside parliament buildings in Jakarta, Indonesia
First Council of Nicaea ends with adoption of the Nicene Creed establishing the doctrine of the Holy Trinity
Roman Senate elects Marcian Eastern Roman Emperor, succeedingTheodosius II, who died without issue
Galileo Galileidemonstrates his first telescope to Venetian lawmakers, including the Doge (ruler) of Venice, Leonardo Donato
TsarPeter the Greatreturns to Moscow after a trip through Western Europe
CaptainJames Cookdeparts from Plymouth, England, bound for the Pacific Ocean on his first voyage aboard the Endeavour
Toussaint Louvertureis imprisoned in Fort de Joux, Jura, France
Paris Salon opens withThéodore Géricault’s painting "The Raft of the Medusa" causing a sensation
The New York Sun publishes the Great Moon Hoax story aboutJohn Herschel, who claimed he had discovered life, landscapes, and fantastical creatures on the Moon through a powerful telescope
British naval officerDavid Beattyis promoted to lieutenant
ChampionJames J. Jeffriesscores a second-round TKO win over Jack Munroe in San Francisco; Jeffries retires after his seventh heavyweight boxing title defense
Ethelda Bleibtreyleads an American medal sweep at the Antwerp Olympics in the women's 100 m freestyle with a world record of 1:13.6, becoming the first US female Olympic champion
AmericanAmelia Earhartcompletes a transcontinental non-stop flight, the first by a woman, landing in Newark, New Jersey
Admiral Lord LouisMountbattenis appointed Supreme Allied Commander in Southeast Asia
"Rashomon," a Japanese film directed byAkira Kurosawaand starringToshiro Mifuneand Machiko Kyō, is released (Honorary Academy Award 1952)
Sugar Ray RobinsonKOs Jose Basora to win middleweight boxing title
Demonstrations against Congolese Prime MinisterPatrice Lumumba
The Beatles go to Bangor, Wales, to study transcendental meditation withMaharishi Mahesh Yogi
Elton Johnmakes his first US appearance at the Troubadour in West Hollywood, Los Angeles
Leader of the Social Democratic and Labour Party,Gerry Fitt, presents several allegations of brutality by the security forces in Northern Ireland to representatives of the United Nations
Columbia Records releasesBruce Springsteen's landmark third album "Born to Run"
Canadian NHL playerTim Hortonposthumously inducted into Hockey Hall of Fame
TV series "Hart to Hart," starringRobert WagnerandStefanie Powers, premieres in the US on ABC
Mets'Dwight Goodenbecomes the youngest pitcher to win 20 games (20 years, 9 months, 9 days)

Warner Bros. Records releasesPaul Simon's seventh solo album "Graceland," a blend of pop, African, zydeco, and rock music; it wins the Grammy for Album of the Year in 1987 and sells over 16 million copies
Carl Lewisruns the 100 m in a world record of 9.86 seconds
Jazz trumpeterMiles Davisgives what becomes his final live performance at the Hollywood Bowl in Los Angeles, California
Linux is born whenLinus Torvaldssends an email announcing his project to create a new computer operating system
Welsh rock bandStereophonicsrelease their debut studio album "Word Gets Around"
"The Miseducation ofLauryn Hill" debut album by Lauryn Hill is released (5 Grammy Awards including Album of the Year, Billboard Album of the Year 1998)
Former Ukrainian Prime MinisterPavlo Lazarenkois sentenced to nine years in federal prison for money laundering, wire fraud, and extortion
PresidentDonald Trumpgrants a presidential pardon to Joe Arpaio, who is convicted of contempt of court for racial profiling
Benny AnderssonandBjörn Ulvaeus' musical "Mamma Mia!," featuring the songs ofABBA, reopens at the Novello Theatre in London's West End after COVID-19 stopped performances in March 2020
CaptainMatthew Webbmakes the first recorded unassisted swim across the English Channel in 21 hours and 45 minutes
Graf Zeppelinpasses over San Francisco en route to Los Angeles after a trans-Pacific voyage

German commander Dietrich von Choltitz surrenders Paris to the Free French forces ofPhilippe Leclerc, disobeying Adolf Hitler's orders to destroy the city. President of the Provisional Government of the French Republic Charles de Gaulle gives a famous speech at the Hôtel de Ville.
Momofuku Andomarkets the first package of precooked instant noodles, Chikin Ramen
Voyager 2's closest approach to Saturn (63,000 miles; 100,000 km)
UN Security Council authorizes military action against Iraq for invading Kuwait
WHO announces that Africa has eradicated polio, defined as 4 years since the last case
